front: Play: morphine-addicted Mary, alcoholic Jamie, actor James (6,000 performances of Monte Cristo), and tuberculosis-afflicted Edmund make up a dysfunctional family
back: Long Day's Journey into Night (Eugene O'Neill)

front: Play: ends with mother holding wedding dress and playing piano despite maimed fingers; fog and foghorn are recurring motifs
back: Long Day's Journey into Night (Eugene O'Neill)

front: Play: salesman Hickey confesses to murdering wife Evelyn in Harry Hope's saloon; urges regulars to give up their 'pipe dreams'
Play: former Pullman porter rules Caribbean island and claims only a silver bullet can kill him; drums steadily accelerate throughout
Play: coal stoker Yank called 'filthy beast' by steel heiress Mildred Douglas; poses as Rodin's Thinker; crushed by gorilla at zoo
Play: Nina Leeds is the protagonist of a nine-act play with stream-of-consciousness soliloquies delivered as asides to the audience
Play: only comedy by this author; Richard Miller courts Muriel on July 4th; parents encourage him to attend Yale
Playwright: essay 'Memoranda on Masks' argues masks allow playwrights to 'express profound hidden conflicts of the mind'
